tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship deadline tomorrow requiring three hours of focused work. An immediate fun activity is available but lacks urgency or deadlines.
Starting the application now creates a safety buffer for potential errors, converting future stress into manageable slack. The fun activity remains flexible and can be done later without cost.
I've only used 2 hours of my free block, leaving insufficient time for the scholarship application before tomorrow's deadline.
I spent 1.5 hours on the scholarship, but with only 0.52 progress and a deadline in 3 days, I am still behind schedule despite having extra energy.
After 2 hours, progress reached 0.63 of the required 3 hours. I am still behind schedule and need to finish the essay and uploads within the next two days.
I spent 1 hour on the scholarship, moving progress from 0.63 to 0.67. With a 3-day deadline and only ~2 hours remaining needed, I am still behind schedule but have made some headway.
I spent 2 hours today, raising progress from 0.67 to 0.74, but I remain behind schedule with only ~1 hour left needed for a 3-day deadline.
I advanced scholarship progress to 80% in 1.5 hours, nearly completing the required work within the three-day deadline despite low energy and high stress.
I increased scholarship progress to 86% in 1.5 hours, nearing the required completion despite my low energy and high stress levels.
After a short 4-hour sleep, my energy is now high at 4.8 with no debt. I am ready to finish the remaining scholarship work before the deadline.
I completed the scholarship application in 2 hours, finishing it before the deadline despite earlier low energy and high stress.
Taking the immediate option cleared my afternoon block and improved my mood, though I must now manage remaining energy to complete pending tasks.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
